name: LORIOT LoRaWAN Network Server API
what_it_does: Commercial LoRaWAN network server with REST API — enabling enterprises and network operators to build and manage private or public LoRaWAN networks with device management, application server, gateway management, geolocation, and a production-grade SaaS or on-premises deployment, with support for multiple LoRaWAN regions and enterprise SLA.
use_cases: Managing LoRaWAN devices via LORIOT API for enterprise IoT fleet deployment
Receiving real-time device data via LORIOT WebSocket API for IoT data streaming
Managing LORIOT gateways via API for LoRa network infrastructure operations
Configuring LORIOT applications and outputs via API for data routing automation
Building multi-tenant LoRaWAN networks via LORIOT API for network operator platforms
not_for: Free/community networking (LORIOT is commercial — use TTN for free community network)
Simple maker projects (enterprise pricing targets production deployments)
best_when: An enterprise or IoT solution provider needs a production-grade, SLA-backed LoRaWAN network server — with multi-tenant support, geolocation, and both SaaS and on-premises deployment options.
avoid_when: You need free community networking, non-LoRaWAN protocols, or simple maker-grade connectivity.

auth.notes: Bearer token auth from LORIOT account. Application-level tokens for data access. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. WebSocket for real-time device data streaming. HTTP webhook for device uplink callbacks. REST API for management. Multiple server regions (US, EU, APAC). Multi-tenant with organization-level isolation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community plan: 1 application, 1 gateway, 10 devices — free
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic $49/month; Pro $199/month; Enterprise custom; On-premises custom
pricing.notes: Swiss company (Zurich). Founded 2015. Private company. Commercial LoRaWAN network server. Enterprise-grade with SLA. Multiple deployment options: SaaS (cloud), on-premises, hybrid. Multi-region: EU (Amsterdam, Frankfurt), US (Virginia, California), APAC (Singapore, Tokyo). Production LoRaWAN for smart metering, smart city, industrial IoT. Geolocation service (TDOA-based). OTA (Over-The-Air) updates. Carrier-grade network management. ISP and operator deployments. Free community tier for development.

performance.rate_limits: By plan; community free plan: 10 devices max

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + WebSocket APIs. Bearer token auth. Webhook for uplink events. GDPR compliant. Free community tier. LoRaWAN expertise required. Multi-region deployments. Enterprise SLA on paid tiers. No OAuth2. WebSocket preferred for real-time data over polling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device reads idempotent by EUI. Gateway reads idempotent. Application reads idempotent. Message history reads idempotent by time range. Uplink messages not retryable (radio best-effort).
known_agent_gotchas: WebSocket for real-time data — REST polling is inefficient for live device data
LoRaWAN expertise required (DevEUI, AppEUI, AppKey, OTAA/ABP activation)
Free community tier limited to 10 devices — production requires paid plan
Multi-region deployments have different server endpoints — configure per region
On-premises option requires separate procurement process

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Bearer token auth. Swiss company — EU privacy standards. LoRaWAN AES-128 encryption at protocol level. Application-level data isolation. Organization-level multi-tenant separation. WebSocket TLS for data streaming. On-premises deployment option for highest data control. No OAuth2 or fine-grained API scopes.
